name: radarr
description: Movie manager for automated downloads and library organization

fields.name: RADARR_PORT
fields.label: App Port
fields.help: Port used to open Radarr in your browser.

fields.name: RADARR_CONFIG_LOCATION
fields.label: Radarr Config Folder
fields.help: Stores Radarr configuration, database, and application state.

fields.name: DOWNLOADS_LOCATION
fields.label: Downloads Folder
fields.help: Folder where your download client saves completed and in-progress movie downloads.

fields.name: MOVIES_LOCATION
fields.label: Movies Library Folder
fields.help: Folder where Radarr will organize and manage your movie library.

fields.name: PUID
fields.label: PUID
fields.help: User ID used by the container for file permissions.

fields.name: PGID
fields.label: PGID
fields.help: Group ID used by the container for file permissions.

fields.name: TZ
fields.label: Timezone
fields.help:
Timezone used by Radarr for scheduled tasks and logs.
Example: Asia/Dubai.

fields.name: RESTART_POLICY
fields.label: Restart Policy
fields.help: Controls whether the container restarts automatically.

fields.name: RADARR_NETWORK_MODE
fields.label: Network
fields.help: Choose Create New Bridge for the default Compose network, Host Mode for host networking, or an existing named Docker network.

fields.name: RADARR_IMAGE
fields.label: Image
fields.help:
Docker image used for Radarr.
Change this only if you want a different tag or image source.

fields.name: RADARR_CONTAINER_NAME
fields.label: Container Name
fields.help: EasyDocker sets the container name from the project name.

ui.overview: Radarr helps you track, download, and organize movies automatically.
what_happens: A Radarr container will be created
Your config, downloads, and movies library folders will be mounted into the container
You can keep the default bridge network or attach Radarr to an existing network
ui.data_location: Relative paths are stored inside this app's project folder under the mounted EasyDocker base config path.
